heading: Senior investor protections
label: 5537 Senior investor protections
notes: CodificationSection was not enacted as part of subtitle C of title X of , which comprises this part.Pub. L. 111–203
Effective DateSection effective 1 day after , except as otherwise provided, see , set out as a note under .July 21, 2010section 4 of Pub. L. 111–203section 5301 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a securities commission (or any agency or office performing like functions) of a State that the Office determines has adopted rules on the appropriate use of designations in the offer or sale of securities or the provision of investment advice that meet or exceed the minimum requirements of the NASAA Model Rule on the Use of Senior-Specific Certifications and Professional Designations (or any successor thereto);

children.label: i
children.text: adopted rules on the appropriate use of designations in the sale of insurance products that, to the extent practicable, conform to the minimum requirements of the National Association of Insurance Commissioners Model Regulation on the Use of Senior-Specific Certifications and Professional Designations in the Sale of Life Insurance and Annuities (or any successor thereto); and

children.label: ii
children.text: adopted rules with respect to fiduciary or suitability requirements in the sale of annuities that meet or exceed the minimum requirements established by the Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation of the National Association of Insurance Commissioners (or any successor thereto); or

children.label: i
children.text: the securities commission (or any agency or office performing like functions) of the State is eligible under subparagraph (A); or

children.label: ii
children.text: the insurance commission (or any agency or office performing like functions) of the State is eligible under subparagraph (B);
the term “financial product” means a security, an insurance product (including an insurance product that pays a return, whether fixed or variable), a bank product, and a loan product;

children.label: A
children.text: means a certification, professional designation, or other purported credential that indicates or implies that a salesperson or adviser has special certification or training in advising or servicing seniors; and

children.label: i
children.text: was issued by or obtained from an academic institution having regional accreditation;

children.label: ii
children.text: meets the standards for certifications and professional designations outlined by the NASAA Model Rule on the Use of Senior-Specific Certifications and Professional Designations (or any successor thereto) or by the Model Regulations on the Use of Senior-Specific Certifications and Professional Designations in the Sale of Life Insurance and Annuities, adopted by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners (or any successor thereto); or

children.label: iii
children.text: was issued by or obtained from a State;
the term “misleading or fraudulent marketing” means the use of a misleading designation by a person that sells to or advises a senior in connection with the sale of a financial product;
the term “NASAA” means the North American Securities Administrators Association;
the term “Office” means the Office of Financial Literacy of the Bureau;
the term “senior” means any individual who has attained the age of 62 years or older; and
the term “State” has the same meaning as in .section 78c(a) of title 15

children.heading: Grants to States for enhanced protection of seniors from being misled by false designations
children.label: b Grants to States for enhanced protection of seniors from being misled by false designations

children.text: to hire staff to identify, investigate, and prosecute (through civil, administrative, or criminal enforcement actions) cases involving misleading or fraudulent marketing;

to fund technology, equipment, and training for regulators, prosecutors, and law enforcement officers, in order to identify salespersons and advisers who target seniors through the use of misleading designations;
to fund technology, equipment, and training for prosecutors to increase the successful prosecution of salespersons and advisers who target seniors with the use of misleading designations;

to provide educational materials and training to regulators on the appropriateness of the use of designations by salespersons and advisers in connection with the sale and marketing of financial products;
to provide educational materials and training to seniors to increase awareness and understanding of misleading or fraudulent marketing;
to develop comprehensive plans to combat misleading or fraudulent marketing of financial products to seniors; and
to enhance provisions of State law to provide protection for seniors against misleading or fraudulent marketing.

children.label: A
children.text: an identification of the scope of the problem of misleading or fraudulent marketing in the State;

children.label: i
children.text: protect seniors from misleading or fraudulent marketing in the sale of financial products, including by proactively identifying victims of misleading and fraudulent marketing who are seniors;

children.label: ii
children.text: assist in the investigation and prosecution of those using misleading or fraudulent marketing; and

children.label: iii
children.text: discourage and reduce cases of misleading or fraudulent marketing; and

children.label: C
children.text: a description of how the proposed activities would be coordinated with other State efforts; and
any other information, as the Office determines is appropriate.

children.heading: Performance objectives and reporting requirements
children.label: d Performance objectives and reporting requirements
children.text: The Office may establish such performance objectives and reporting requirements for States and eligible entities receiving a grant under this section as the Office determines are necessary to carry out and assess the effectiveness of the program under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: on the appropriate use of designations in the offer or sale of securities or investment advice that meet or exceed the minimum requirements of the NASAA Model Rule on the Use of Senior-Specific Certifications and Professional Designations (or any successor thereto);

children.label: B
children.text: on the appropriate use of designations in the sale of insurance products that, to the extent practicable, conform to the minimum requirements of the National Association of Insurance Commissioners Model Regulation on the Use of Senior-Specific Certifications and Professional Designations in the Sale of Life Insurance and Annuities (or any successor thereto); and

children.label: C
children.text: with respect to fiduciary or suitability requirements in the sale of annuities that meet or exceed the minimum requirements established by the Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation of the National Association of Insurance Commissioners (or any successor thereto); and

children.label: A
children.text: rules on the appropriate use of designations in the offer or sale of securities or investment advice that meet or exceed the minimum requirements of the NASAA Model Rule on the Use of Senior-Specific Certifications and Professional Designations (or any successor thereto); or

children.label: i
children.text: on the appropriate use of designations in the sale of insurance products that, to the extent practicable, conform to the minimum requirements of the National Association of Insurance Commissioners Model Regulation on the Use of Senior-Specific Certifications and Professional Designations in the Sale of Life Insurance and Annuities (or any successor thereto); and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to fiduciary or suitability requirements in the sale of annuities that meet or exceed the minimum requirements established by the Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation of the National Association of Insurance Commissioners (or any successor thereto).

children.heading: Subgrants
children.label: f Subgrants
children.text: A State or eligible entity that receives a grant under this section may make a subgrant, as the State or eligible entity determines is necessary to carry out the activities funded using a grant under this section.

children.heading: Reapplication
children.label: g Reapplication
children.text: A State or eligible entity that receives a grant under this section may reapply for a grant under this section, notwithstanding the limitations on grant amounts under subsection (e).

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section, $8,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2011 through 2015.
